2. Tech Research:
**Market**: Competitors (Proloquo2Go: 300k users/$250 OT; TouchChat: 100k/$300; Avaz: 50k/$100/yr; First Then: 200k/$10). Trends: $200-300 OT or $10-15/mo subs; pilots -> scale via grants. Position: $50 OT/$5/mo undercut.

**Tech Stack**: React Native/PWA (UI/gestures), Render PostgreSQL (managed DB) + Render Web Service (Express API, cookie sessions, OAuth), Expo (builds $0-29/mo), Dexie/IndexedDB (offline), Web Speech TTS/haptics, ARASAAC symbols + teacher uploads/Canvas transform (no copyright issue w/ consent). Stable Diffusion API (custom imgs). Charts: Victory. Total MVP: 6-8wks, $0-90/mo no clients, break-even Mo1 w/ 20 schools @ $50 ea.
3. Features and KPIs:
8 core features grouped into 4 modules (2 per module), prioritized must-haves first. Technical dev KPIs for MVP (testable milestones: offline function, touch accuracy, data persistence).

**Module 1: Core Communication & Routines (MUST-HAVE)**
- **AAC Symbol Grid**: 1. 100+ drag-drop icons + TTS (95% playback accuracy). 2. Sentence builder UI (80% drag success). 3. Offline custom phrases (sync test).
- **Visual Schedules**: 1. Drag timeline with photos (85% reorder). 2. Timers/first-then (95% alerts). 3. Check-off/audio (offline save).

**Module 2: Interaction & Self-Awareness (MUST-HAVE)**
- **PECS-Style Exchange**: 1. Card swap UI (90% gestures). 2. Request log/rewards (100% persistence). 3. CSV import (70% <1s).
- **Emotion ID Tools**: 1. Matching grid (85% touch). 2. Haptic feedback (compatibility test). 3. Stats retention.

**Module 3: Personalization & Calm (GOOD-TO-HAVE)**
- **Social Stories Creator**: 1. Drag templates (80% preview). 2. Voiceover (90% export). 3. PDF share.
- **Sensory Breaks**: 1. Timers/visuals (100% offline). 2. Auto-pause (battery test). 3. Log export (<2s).

**Module 4: Oversight & Reliability (GOOD-TO-HAVE)**
- **Teacher/Parent Dashboard**: 1. Multi-login/sync (95% realtime). 2. KPI charts (mobile render). 3. Exports (privacy).
- **Offline Mode + Simplicity**: 1. Full caching (100% post-disconnect). 2. 48px UI (low-end test). 3. 90% stability.
4. Pages and navigation:
12 lean pages. Kid: Bottom tabs (visual). Adult: Top tabs (data). Flows: Landing -> Login (role select) -> Home -> Modules (swipe/back) -> Offline fallback.

**Public**: 1. Landing (hero/demo/signup). 2. Login/Register (email, GitHub, onboard).
**Kid Flow**: 3. Kid Home (module carousel/schedule peek). 4. AAC Grid. 5. Visual Schedules. 6. PECS. 7. Emotion ID. 8. Social Stories. 9. Sensory Breaks.
**Adult Flow**: 10. Dashboard (KPIs/charts/kid select). 11. Kid Profile Editor. 12. Settings (themes/offline/logout).

Industry standard: Grid layouts, calm blues/greens, big icons, haptic, 1-action screens (like Proloquo/TouchChat).
5. UI/UX design: Nebula UI Studio workflow (canonical). After the user answers the first question about the app, Grok 4 directly sends a high-quality prompt to Pencil / Nebula UI Studio to generate the complete UI. Grok does not ask for approval before generating the UI. The generated UI is directly used in the app.
6. Environment Setup:
## Render workspaces & internal identity (canonical)

**Main account:** All automated provisioning runs under the Nebula Render account **nebulla.dev.ai@gmail.com**. End users never obtain or see Render credentials.

**Trigger:** When a user **creates a project in Nebula**, the control plane **must automatically** create a **dedicated Render workspace** for that tenant (or attach the project to the workspace already reserved for that tenant—same rule: one workspace per internal client boundary).

**Permanent client ID:** The **`workspace_id`** returned by Render is the **only** permanent internal client identifier. **Do not** generate a parallel random client ID. All server-side resolution is **internal client ID (workspace) → resources**.

**Scope of resources:** Inside that workspace only: **Web Services**, **PostgreSQL**, **workers/cron**, and **every** environment variable and secret for that client. No cross-workspace tenant data.

**Never expose:** Workspace ID and internal client ID are **vault / encrypted store / server config only**. They must **not** appear in the browser, URLs, JWTs to the client, chat, client-visible Master Plan fields, Nebula UI Studio exports, or public logs. Operators see **opaque project labels**; raw IDs are infra-restricted.

**Public surface:** **nebulla.dev** URLs and **project name** in UI—nothing that reveals Render workspace or internal client ID.

---

## UI & product source of truth

Use approved output from **`nebula-sysh-ui-sysh-studio.md`** (`NEBULA_UI_STUDIO_CODE`) and **`nebulla-sysh-ui-sysh-studio/approved/approved-ui.svg`** for layout, screens, and components when planning implementation.

---

## Delivery phases (MVP)

**A — Control plane:** Idempotent create-workspace on project create; persist workspace_id ↔ tenant mapping; retries and health checks.

**B — Data plane:** PostgreSQL and `DATABASE_URL` only inside that workspace; migrations; backups.

**C — App runtime:** Web Service (API + SPA), cookie sessions, email + GitHub auth; all deploy config and secrets scoped to that workspace.

**D — Product loop:** Master Plan → Mind Map → Nebula UI Studio → code; observability (e.g. Guardian) namespaced so tenant IDs stay opaque externally.

**E — Hardening:** Staging/production separation (services or env lanes), runbooks, regression checks that workspace/client IDs never leak to end users.

---

## User, project, and secrets model (additive)

- Each Render workspace belongs to **one user** (single owner boundary).
- A **single user may create multiple projects**.
- **Every project must have its own project ID** (unique and persistent in the product data model).
- **Every project has its own isolated** environment variables, secrets, and API keys—no cross-project sharing unless explicitly designed and audited.
- **UI work:** Always reference **`nebula-sysh-sysh-studio.md`** for the design system when implementing or reviewing UI (use together with `SKILL.md` and approved UI assets where applicable).

---

## Project creation & env vars (additive, explicit)

When a user **creates a project**, execute the following in order. **Do not assume** hostnames, ports, or URLs—read every value from Render or generate it as specified.

**Step 1 — New Render workspace:** Create a **new Render workspace** under the main Nebula Render account **nebulla.dev.ai@gmail.com** (control-plane automation with stored credentials; end users never perform this in Render).

**Step 2 — Client ownership:** That workspace **belongs to that client** (the tenant for the user who created the project). Persist Render’s **`workspace_id`** and treat that workspace as the boundary for all infra for that client unless product rules say otherwise.

**Step 3 — Multiple projects per workspace:** A **client workspace may contain multiple projects**. **Each project** must receive its **own unique `project_id`** in Nebula’s data model (separate from `workspace_id` and from Render service IDs).

**Step 4 — Environment variables (per new project / deployment):** For **each** new project, open the **Environment** (or **Environment Variables**) section for **that project’s** Render Web Service (or the target service you deploy). Configure:

- **Copied from Nebula canonical `.env` (nebulla-v3 repo)** — same Nebula-owned vendor keys; paste **exact** values, no placeholders:
  - `GROK_API_KEY`
  - `PENCIL_API_KEY` (and `PENCIL_API_URL` only if nebulla-v3 uses a non-default mockups base URL)

- **Created fresh per project** — never reuse another project’s values:
  - `DATABASE_URL` — Create (or select) a **PostgreSQL** instance **inside this workspace**. In Render → Postgres → **Connect** / connection info, copy the **Internal** or **External** URL string Render displays for **that** instance. Paste it verbatim into `DATABASE_URL`. Do not fabricate host, user, password, or database name.
  - `SESSION_SECRET` — Generate a **new** strong random secret for **this project only** (minimum 16 characters; prefer 32+ from a CSPRNG). Store only in Render env / vault.
  - `PUBLIC_SITE_URL` — After the Web Service has a public URL, set this to the **exact** HTTPS origin Render shows for **this** service (Dashboard → Web Service → **URL** / custom domain). Do not use another environment’s URL or a guessed `onrender.com` name before Render assigns it.

**Step 5 — Validate:** Save env, trigger deploy if needed, then verify health (`/api/health`), DB connectivity, OAuth/email links against the configured `PUBLIC_SITE_URL`, and that secrets are not exposed in client bundles.

---

## Secrets and Integrations → Render env (standard flow)

**Dashboard → Secrets and Integrations:** Any **API key, token, or secret** the user adds, edits, or removes there for the **active project** must be **automatically mirrored** to that **same project’s** Render Web Service **environment variables** (key names aligned with what the runtime expects—document the mapping in the control plane). This is part of **standard project creation** (initial sync after first deploy target exists) and **standard project update** (every change on that page triggers an idempotent sync to Render; removals delete or null the corresponding env per policy).

**Never** treat Dashboard secrets as browser-only for production: the source of truth for deployed runtime is **Render env** for that project’s service; Nebula UI is the editor, Render is where the process reads secrets.

**Planning read order (agents & implementers):** **Before** starting work on a project, **while** reading the Master Plan, and **after** finishing the Master Plan pass, also review the user’s **Secrets and Integrations** entries for that project so the **Environment Setup (Tab 6)** and Render env worklists include **every** integration the user configured—do not assume missing keys in code or in Tab 6 if the user already supplied them on that page.
